name: Config reload during traffic

injection.description: Publish a gateway configuration change (routing weights, limits, tenant tiers) while streams are active and load is steady.

expected_gateway_semantics: The new configuration applies as an atomic snapshot swap: in-flight requests complete under the snapshot they started with; new requests use the new snapshot.
Zero streams are dropped, reset, or errored by the reload.
The inference.config_version trace attribute changes at the swap, and the new config is effective within the config-publish SLO (<= 5s).
expected_client_visible_behavior: No client-visible interruption: active streams continue uninterrupted, no errors, no reconnects, no latency step attributable to the reload.

metrics_that_must_move.expectation: continues increasing with status_class=2xx through the swap window (traffic never pauses)

metrics_that_must_not_move.expectation: no status_class=5xx increase attributable to the reload window
no discontinuity (drop to zero or spike) at the swap — streams were not dropped or re-established

abort_condition: Abort the injection (and roll back the config) if any active stream terminates without normal completion during the swap window, or if the new configuration is not effective within 5s of publish.
